title: China-to-Colombia Solar PV Inverter Compliance Gap Matrix
provenance: AI-compiled, multi-model cross-checked, not human-verified. Informational only; confirm with the official regulator and a qualified professional. Not legal/certification/customs/market-access advice.

rows.target_requirement: Colombia is a 60 Hz grid market. PV inverter files should show the exact Colombia firmware profile, nominal frequency, trip windows, reconnection time, anti-islanding behavior, power-quality limits, and commissioning lock method required by the network operator and project design. Review CREG 025/1995 together with later CREG instruments commonly cited for large-scale and small-scale generation connection, including Resolución CREG 060 de 2019 and Resolución CREG 174 de 2021 where the project scope fits.
rows.china_equivalent: China domestic grid-connected PV inverter settings are normally configured for 50 Hz operation. A dual-frequency hardware claim is not enough; the shipped unit should have verified 60 Hz firmware, protection parameters, and site commissioning records for Colombia.
rows.gap: The key gap is settings control. Exporters should not ship a China 50 Hz default profile and leave the project to adjust undocumented parameters in the field.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid connection because installed settings must match the Colombian network operator and project interconnection approval
mandatory_status.cn: China domestic settings are normally 50 Hz and are not Colombian settings

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Confirm and lock the Colombia 60 Hz profile before shipment or commissioning. A China 50 Hz type test does not prove Colombian grid-setting compliance.

source.publisher: Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas (CREG)

rows.requirement: China GB/T Baseline Versus Colombian Acceptance
rows.target_requirement: Colombian acceptance is built around RETIE, CREG interconnection, project-owner requirements, and any applicable NTC / IEC evidence route. China GB/T inverter and PV power-station reports may help identify technical capability, but they are not Colombian legal instruments.
rows.china_equivalent: China baseline files commonly reference GB/T 19964-2024 for PV power-station grid connection, GB/T 37408-2019 for grid-connected PV inverter technical requirements, and NB/T 32004-2018 for grid-connected PV inverter technical specification. These are useful baseline documents but do not create automatic recognition in Colombia.
rows.gap: Map each China report to a Colombian requirement rather than presenting the China certificate as equivalent. The mapping should identify which clauses support RETIE safety, which support CREG grid review, and which remain uncovered.

mandatory_status.target: Colombian RETIE / CREG / project requirements are controlling for Colombian deployment
mandatory_status.cn: GB/T and NB/T evidence may be mandatory or project-specific in China, but is only supporting evidence for Colombia

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] China GB/T evidence is a baseline, not a Colombian substitute. Build a clause-by-clause bridge to RETIE, CREG, and NTC / IEC requirements.

source.publisher: State Administration for Market Regulation of China (SAMR)

rows.requirement: CREG Distributed Generation and Grid-Connection Route
rows.target_requirement: Colombia's CREG rules for self-generation and distributed generation create a project-side interconnection route. For inverter exports, the compliance file should support the Colombian customer or EPC with model data, protection settings, anti-islanding evidence, single-line diagrams, commissioning records, and utility-facing documentation.
target_standards: CREG rules for AGPE, GD, and distributed energy resource connection
rows.china_equivalent: China grid evidence typically references GB/T 19964, GB/T 37408, NB/T 32004, and domestic utility commissioning records. Those documents are useful for engineering comparison but do not replace the Colombian utility interconnection process.
rows.gap: The inverter supplier should provide Colombian-grid parameter sheets and evidence in a format the project owner can submit to the network operator. A China grid profile, even when technically robust, is not a Colombian interconnection approval.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory for grid-connected projects within the CREG self-generation or distributed-generation route; details depend on capacity, connection point, network operator, and project category
mandatory_status.cn: Mandatory for domestic grid connection through Chinese utility processes, but not transferable to Colombia

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] CREG grid acceptance is project-specific. Prepare Colombian interconnection evidence instead of relying on China utility records.

source.publisher: Comisión de Regulación de Energía y Gas (CREG)

rows.requirement: ICONTEC / NTC Safety Evidence Based on IEC 62109
rows.target_requirement: PV inverter safety files for Colombia commonly need evidence aligned to IEC 62109 / NTC IEC 62109: Part 1 covers general safety requirements for PV power conversion equipment, and Part 2 covers particular inverter requirements. The Colombian acceptance route should confirm the exact NTC edition, certification body, and whether CB reports are accepted as supporting evidence.
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese manufacturers often hold IEC 62109 CB certificates or test reports for export models. Those reports can be relevant, but the Colombian file still needs Spanish documentation, model identity control, local certification-body acceptance, and any RETIE-linked marking or inspection evidence.
rows.gap: Do not treat an IEC 62109 report as automatic Colombian approval. The report should be mapped to the exact NTC / RETIE route, model name, firmware, voltage class, environmental rating, and installation manual used in Colombia.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory only when incorporated into the applicable RETIE / certification route or required by the project owner, utility, or certification body; otherwise a safety evidence route rather than a standalone import licence
mandatory_status.cn: Often voluntary or export-market evidence unless required by a customer, CB scheme route, or destination-market certification

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] IEC 62109 evidence is central for inverter safety, but Colombian acceptance depends on the NTC / RETIE route and certification-body review.

rows.target_requirement: Ley 1715 regulates the integration of non-conventional renewable energy into Colombia's energy system. For PV inverter exports, this is mainly a project and incentive context: the product file may need to support the customer with technical descriptions, origin and customs data, and project documentation, but Ley 1715 is not itself a standalone inverter safety certificate.
target_standards: Colombia non-conventional renewable energy integration framework
rows.china_equivalent: China-side PV inverter certificates, export documents, and HS-code evidence can help a Colombian importer assemble a project file, but they do not prove eligibility for Colombian renewable-energy incentives or tax treatment without the Colombian authority route.
rows.gap: Keep Ley 1715 separate from product conformity. The inverter file should be accurate enough for a Colombian project application, but any incentive, customs, or tax conclusion belongs to the Colombian project owner and authorities.

mandatory_status.target: Not a universal product-certification requirement; mandatory only where a project seeks rights, procedures, or benefits under the renewable-energy framework
mandatory_status.cn: No direct China product-certification equivalent; export documents are supporting evidence only

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Ley 1715 supports renewable-energy project treatment, not automatic inverter market access. Keep incentive documentation separate from RETIE and CREG compliance.

source.publisher: Departamento Administrativo de la Función Pública

rows.requirement: RETIE Electrical Safety and Installation Scope
rows.target_requirement: Colombia's current RETIE framework is tied to Resolución 40117 de 2024 from MinEnergía. For PV inverter projects, the practical file should determine whether the inverter, protection devices, conductors, labels, installation design, inspection, and energisation route fall within RETIE certification or inspection requirements before sale or installation.
target_standards: Resolución 40117 de 2024 (Ministerio de Minas y Energía)
Ministerio de Minas y Energía RETIE technical-regulation framework
rows.china_equivalent: Chinese inverter files commonly include factory test reports, IEC 62109 CB evidence, GB/T 37408 or NB/T 32004 reports, and Chinese labels/manuals. These can support technical review but do not replace Colombian RETIE scope analysis, product certification where required, or installation inspection.
rows.gap: The gap is Colombian legal scope and acceptance, not only electrical test performance. Exporters should prepare model-level certificates, Spanish technical files, labels, installation instructions, protection-device coordination, and project inspection evidence for the RETIE route.

mandatory_status.target: Mandatory where RETIE product or installation scope applies; exact certification and inspection path depends on product, voltage, use, and project configuration
mandatory_status.cn: China factory tests and voluntary certificates do not create Colombian RETIE compliance

rows.verdict: [INFORMATIONAL] Treat RETIE as a separate Colombian gate. A China GB/T or generic IEC report is useful evidence but is not a RETIE certificate or installation approval.

source.publisher: Ministerio de Minas y Energía de Colombia
